description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline that includes the docker-compose-common.yml file and defines two Docker services: celery-worker and django-server. Use the Dockerfile at docker/Dockerfile with build context ..; set the celery-worker target to celery-worker-nosrc and the django-server target to django-server-dbg. Both services load env files secret_locations.env, common.env, a-dev.env, mount a secrets volume at /run/secrets (read‑only) and bind the repository root to /home/container/workdir (read‑write). The django-server runs '/bin/sh -c "python docker/wait_for/wait_for_postgres_60.py && python manage.py migrate && python -m debugpy --listen 0.0.0.0:5678 manage.py runserver 0.0.0.0:8000"' and exposes ports ${DJANGO_PORT:-8000}:8000 and ${DEBUGPY_PORT:-5678}:5678. No explicit jobs or stages are defined; the pipeline relies on these services for integration testing.
